diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Dysplasia epiphysealis hemimelica (Trevor’s disease) of the acetabulum
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical symptoms, including left inguinal pain and moderate pain at the final point of internal rotation, along with imaging findings. Conventional X-rays and CT scans showed asymmetrical ossification proceeding from the left acetabulum, joint incongruency, and discreet lateralisation of the femoral head. Histological investigation of the tumor confirmed an osteochondroma.
